continuation_guard.next_required_action: Operator runs `claude auth login`; resume this run to execute the bounded live Claude canary, exact-tip readiness, and regular-merge landing.

acceptance.criterion: Fresh Claude and Codex bundles run help, config validation, doctor, setup dry-run, onboard plan, implement status, and OpenRouter help without import errors or model calls.
acceptance.evidence: Installed-artifact smoke passed all 12 Claude/Codex cases on Python 3.12 and 3.14; full exact-tip suites passed 852 tests on each interpreter.

acceptance.criterion: Adding a runtime module requires no manual copy-list edit; removing any imported runtime module makes installed-artifact smoke fail.
acceptance.evidence: Recursive installer/audit mutation coverage passed in tests/test_install_audit.py and tests/test_installed_artifact_smoke.py.

acceptance.criterion: All advertised built-in adapters retain their canonical adapter and I/O contract.
acceptance.evidence: Canonical adapter registry and route-contract coverage passed in the 852-test Python 3.12/3.14 matrix.

acceptance.criterion: CI policy proves runtime-only changes trigger validation and recursively checks Python, shell, JSON, consistency, release, and installed bundles.
acceptance.evidence: GitHub Repo Consistency passed Ubuntu 3.10/3.12/3.14 and macOS 3.12 at afec3e4; workflow/verify_repo contract tests passed locally.

acceptance.criterion: Claude receives exactly seven marked aliases; Codex receives `SKILL.md` and `AGENTS.md` with no Claude alias tree.
acceptance.evidence: Installed-bundle audit and host-layout tests passed on Python 3.12 and 3.14.

batches.name: Canonical Behavior Policy and Full-Run Supervisor

acceptance.criterion: Semantic scenario tests resolve expected routing, continuation, landing, test-integrity, and rollback behavior without relying on literal phrase presence.
acceptance.evidence: Structured behavior-policy and contradiction fixtures passed in both 852-test interpreter runs.

acceptance.criterion: A fake Grok completes multiple batches from one background launch with no `resume-batch` call, then produces ordered events and one valid final report.
acceptance.evidence: Full-run fixture integration passed; the disposable real Grok canary also completed two ordered batches in one launch.

acceptance.criterion: Launch returns promptly; monitor detects healthy, complete, failed, blocked, and stale states; stop terminates only the exact fingerprinted supervisor domain and never signals a cached numeric process group.
acceptance.evidence: Supervisor lifecycle, refusal, descendant, PID-reuse, and stale-state regressions passed on Python 3.12/3.14; real canary exited its exact process group with code 0.

acceptance.criterion: Status JSON never contains the raw transcript or secret values, and transcript/event artifacts have private permissions.
acceptance.evidence: Bounded status/redaction/permission tests and live secret scan passed; real canary monitor exposed only bounded event/report paths and state.

acceptance.criterion: Once healthy, the driver contract is `parked_monitor` with only the enumerated wake conditions, no per-push or per-tool-call re-entry, explicit quiet polling, silent unchanged-health snapshots, and host-coalesced nonterminal user updates.
acceptance.evidence: Supervisor policy tests passed and the real Grok canary remained parked through its healthy batch transition until final_readiness.

batches.name: Exact Session Identity and Atomic Writer Qualification

acceptance.criterion: Colliding and traversal-shaped IDs cannot collide, escape runtime storage, or retrieve a record whose embedded ID differs.
acceptance.evidence: Digest-key, traversal, symlink/hardlink, embedded-ID, and duplicate-alias regressions passed in both full suites.

acceptance.criterion: Concurrent session/lease operations leave valid JSON and allow exactly one live lease; malformed records block rather than disappear.
acceptance.evidence: Atomic CAS/locking, concurrent lease, malformed-store, and one-read provenance tests passed on Python 3.12/3.14.

acceptance.criterion: Read-only commands do not create runtime directories; arbitrary/unregistered sessions cannot resume or acquire write authority.
acceptance.evidence: Read-only store and legacy/unregistered write-authority regressions passed in both full suites.

acceptance.criterion: Missing, mismatched, stale, preference-declared, or unsupported qualification evidence fails closed while an exact qualified fixture succeeds.
acceptance.evidence: Strict qualification/resume lifecycle and exact route/model/worktree/CWD/parent/HEAD/sandbox fixtures passed on Python 3.12/3.14.

batches.name: External Isolation, Environment, and Redaction Boundary

acceptance.criterion: A hostile fixture lane cannot read ignored `.env`, repository instructions, host-home sentinels, or sibling worktrees, while relevant tracked source remains readable.
acceptance.evidence: Tracked-snapshot, canonical-path, hardlink, Darwin dylib-alias, sibling-worktree, and hostile-lane isolation regressions passed.

acceptance.criterion: Optional external isolation failure falls back native; a required route blocks without launching and never degrades to repo-root access.
acceptance.evidence: Required/optional isolation and Darwin capability fallback tests passed in the full dual-interpreter matrix.

acceptance.criterion: A fake implementer sees only explicit environment grants; unrelated credential sentinels are absent and descendants are gone after timeout.
acceptance.evidence: Minimal environment, credential-grant, timeout, process-group, and descendant cleanup regressions passed; real Grok auth/push canary completed under isolated HOME.

acceptance.criterion: Secret-shaped values never survive in JSON, artifacts, CLI output, notifications, webhook/remote logs, or humanized errors.
acceptance.evidence: Full secret scanner passed 34 tests on each interpreter plus a live repository scan; structured malformed-input and auth metadata outputs remained redacted.

acceptance.criterion: No temporary lane directory survives any tested exit path.
acceptance.evidence: Success/failure/timeout/cancel cleanup regressions passed and the disposable real Grok worktree/runtime/refs were removed after terminal reconciliation.

batches.name: Durable Audit, Delegated Git, and Exact Landing Evidence

acceptance.criterion: Audit pass is persisted; missing, stale, failed, reordered, or tampered evidence cannot export, and no state transition implicitly promotes an audit.
acceptance.evidence: Worker-audit state/digest/export tamper and transition regressions passed on Python 3.12/3.14.

acceptance.criterion: A dependent two-commit patch series passes cumulatively in order and fails reversed, without changing host refs, index, or worktree.
acceptance.evidence: Cumulative binary patch-chain and reversed-order failure tests passed in both full suites.

acceptance.criterion: A bare-remote integration test proves multiple worker commits/pushes advance only the staged feature branch; base movement, force/non-descendant advance, merge, tag, or second-PR attempts fail.
acceptance.evidence: Delegated-Git integration tests passed; real GitHub canary advanced only codex/grok-push-canary-f47af81 through two commits while main and PR refs stayed unchanged.

acceptance.criterion: Concurrent runs produce distinct rollback refs; report reconciliation rejects wrong session/branch/head and preserves host control fields.
acceptance.evidence: Rollback/ref/reconciliation regressions passed; live canary used a run-scoped rollback ref, reconciled exact identity, then cleaned both remote refs.

acceptance.criterion: Unrelated “tests green” evidence, duplicates, swapped batches, altered text, missing Master Acceptance, and unparseable plans fail; exact criterion mappings pass.
acceptance.evidence: Landing-check stable-ID and adversarial mapping suite passed inside both 852-test full runs.

acceptance.criterion: The former monolithic attempt path becomes a thin coordinator with separately tested components and no behavior regression across native, CLI, timeout, artifact, and failure fixtures.
acceptance.evidence: Dispatch decomposition and native/CLI/timeout/artifact/failure characterization tests passed in the dual-interpreter full matrix.

acceptance.criterion: Consistency behavior is driven by structured policy/scenarios, and contradictory single-kickoff or host-routing variants make tests fail even if preferred phrases remain present.
acceptance.evidence: Separated consistency policy/scenario mutation tests passed; final repository consistency CI is green on all four platform/interpreter jobs.

acceptance.criterion: Identical HEAD/config reuses a passing preflight; source/config/tool changes invalidate it; final readiness never accepts cached evidence alone.
acceptance.evidence: Preflight cache key/reuse/invalidation/final-readiness regressions passed on Python 3.12/3.14.

acceptance.criterion: Review planning is deterministic from evidence, logs why checks were selected/skipped, and escalates security/runtime/high-risk or final changes to the broad gate.
acceptance.evidence: Evidence-aware review planner tests passed and final exact-tip review used the broad 852-test gates plus independent cumulative review.

acceptance.criterion: The API helper produces stable snapshots and blocks an unapproved public-surface break while allowing internal-only changes.
acceptance.evidence: Public-API snapshot/compatibility/approval-manifest tests passed in both full suites and strict CI at afec3e4.

batches.name: Canonical Claude Code/Codex Documentation and Information Architecture

acceptance.criterion: Every canonical surface describes one full-run contract: one persistent worker, feature-branch progress, parked driver, bounded milestone/heartbeat updates, and final host review.
acceptance.evidence: Canonical docs/behavior-policy consistency tests passed; fresh cumulative review found no remaining code/doc finding at afec3e4.

acceptance.criterion: Survival validation conditionally requires the full delegated Run Control fields and records the same semantics for Claude Code and Codex.
acceptance.evidence: Delegated survival-guide validation and host-parity fixtures passed on Python 3.12/3.14.

acceptance.criterion: No canonical surface retains the contradictory second-launch requirement, absolute test immutability/count rule, global rollback tags, or per-batch driver loop as full-run default.
acceptance.evidence: Forbidden-corpus and semantic contradiction tests passed in both exact-tip full suites.

acceptance.criterion: README reaches installation and default usage before optional provider detail; all anchors and installed relative links resolve; Claude alias instructions cover exactly seven.
acceptance.evidence: README information-architecture, Markdown-link, installed-relative-link, and seven-alias checks passed locally and in CI.

acceptance.criterion: Alias, reference, example, config, durable AI-doc, and host-invocation consistency tests all pass.
acceptance.evidence: Repository consistency and installed artifact matrices passed on Python 3.12/3.14 and all required GitHub jobs.

batches.name: v2.1.0 Release Proof, Review, and Landing

acceptance.criterion: `2.1.0` agrees across SKILL, AGENTS, README, changelog, and release checker; Unreleased remains available for future changes.
acceptance.evidence: Release 2.1.0 metadata/changelog checks passed locally and in the four-job Repo Consistency matrix at afec3e4.

acceptance.criterion: Canonical verification, recursive compile, shell/JSON/link/secret/API checks, both unittest modes where still intentionally supported, and both installed-artifact smokes are green.
acceptance.evidence: Exact product tip afec3e4 passed 852 tests on Python 3.14 and 852 on Python 3.12 (12 expected skips each), dual installed smokes, secret scan, and all required GitHub checks.

acceptance.criterion: API inspection resolves literal local helper delegation without hiding dynamic helpers, and strict verification accepts only the documented 2.1.0 `session list` exit-1 change.
acceptance.evidence: Public API/verify regression matrix passed; tracked api-break-approvals.json authorizes only the documented 2.1.0 session-list exit change.

acceptance.criterion: Live Claude Code, Codex, and Grok canaries are recorded without exposing credentials or mutating user-installed skills.
acceptance.evidence: Live Codex passed and the two-batch real GitHub Grok canary passed; Claude Code 2.1.207 is installed but `claude auth status --json` reports loggedIn=false/authMethod=none, so its live invocation is blocked pending operator login.

acceptance.criterion: Worktree is clean, PR is not draft, required checks are green, every review/comment surface has been read, and no requested changes or blocking findings remain.
acceptance.evidence: All required checks are green at 2e9edc8, the worktree is clean, and every review surface is clean except a nonblocking Gemini quota notice; PR #67 intentionally remains draft behind the Claude-live gate.

acceptance.criterion: Final cumulative review is clean, the PR is eligible for `gh pr merge --merge`, and no squash, rebase, tag, release publication, or force push has occurred. The actual regular merge commit is the terminal post-readiness outcome, not evidence required by the pre-merge gate.
acceptance.evidence: Native cumulative review and a pinned read-only Sakana/Fugu review are clean; Claude auth/live proof, draft state, and the resulting exact-tip readiness gate still block merge eligibility.

master_acceptance.criterion: A Claude Code or Codex driver can select trusted Grok full-run delegation and park after one launch while Grok advances the feature branch through all batches.
master_acceptance.evidence: Real Grok/GitHub canary session 2bc6a796-36bf-4ca5-9f8e-bde588aed1e7 completed two batches and two feature-branch pushes from one launch while monitor stayed parked.

master_acceptance.criterion: Driver-visible telemetry is bounded and event-driven; raw worker output stays outside driver context unless explicitly requested.
master_acceptance.evidence: Live monitor used 11 bounded events and a structured report only; unchanged healthy polls were silent and no transcript tail was read.

master_acceptance.criterion: Fresh installed artifacts work for both supported hosts and every advertised adapter, runtime, alias, and relative reference is present and validated.
master_acceptance.evidence: All 12 fresh installed-bundle smoke cases passed for Claude Code and Codex on Python 3.12 and 3.14.

master_acceptance.criterion: Session/lease/audit/isolation/redaction/landing boundaries fail closed under the confirmed collision, traversal, concurrency, secret, stale-evidence, and patch-chain cases.
master_acceptance.evidence: Exact-tip 852-test dual-interpreter suites, secret scanner, CodeQL, Socket, and fresh native cumulative review all passed.

master_acceptance.criterion: All current documentation is host-honest and semantically consistent, the repository is versioned as v2.1.0, and the reviewed PR is clean, green, and eligible for a regular merge commit. Record the actual merge as the terminal post-readiness outcome.
master_acceptance.evidence: Docs/version/checks plus native and pinned Sakana reviews are clean; Claude live proof, ready-for-review state, and the resulting exact-tip landing gate remain.
